text:
BREAST BIOPSY - LEFT UPPER OUTER QUADRANT

Clinical Information: 47-year-old female with palpable mass in left upper outer quadrant.

Specimen: The specimen consists of multiple fragments of tan-yellow tissue measuring approximately 2.5 cm in aggregate. Part A (needle core biopsy): tan tissue measuring 1.5 cm. Part B (excisional biopsy): firm, tan-white tissue measuring 3 x 2 x 1.5 cm with a central firm grey-white focus.

Microscopic Findings: Infiltrating ductal carcinoma with sections showing neoplastic cells arranged in nests and trabeculae. The cells have moderate nuclear enlargement and increased mitotic activity. Some areas show central necrosis. Surrounding tissue shows benign breast parenchyma with scattered lymphocytes.

Histologic Grade: The tumor shows features consistent with Nottingham grade 2 (intermediate grade) carcinoma. Nuclear grade 2, mitotic rate 8-10 per 10 high power fields, tubule formation 2.

Immunophenotype: Positive for cytokeratin, negative for triple negative markers.

Marginal Status: Tumor is present at the inked margin in Part B.

Additional Features: Associated with ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS).

RIGHT BREAST EXCISIONAL BIOPSY - LATERAL ASPECT

Patient: 52-year-old female, screening mammography showed suspicious microcalcifications.

Specimen Description: Tan-white breast tissue measuring 4 x 3 x 2.5 cm with associated nipple-areolar complex. Part A (medial margin): tissue 2 x 1.5 x 1 cm. Part B (lateral margin): tissue 2.5 x 2 x 1.5 cm. Part C (superior margin): tissue 1.5 x 1 x 0.8 cm.

Macroscopic Findings: In Part B, there is a firm, grey-white focus measuring 1.2 cm in greatest dimension. The surrounding tissue is tan-yellow.

Microscopic examination reveals: Invasive lobular carcinoma with cells arranged in single file pattern infiltrating through breast tissue. Grade 1 (well differentiated). Associated extensive high-grade DCIS. Stromal response shows moderate desmoplasia.

All margins are clear of tumor (minimum 2 mm).

Additional Testing: Lymphovascular invasion not identified. No involvement of nipple-areolar complex.

SENTINEL LYMPH NODE AND BREAST TISSUE BIOPSY

Clinical History: 61-year-old female status post breast conservation therapy, now undergoing re-excision for positive margins.

Specimen: Part A: Sentinel lymph node measuring 1.2 x 0.8 x 0.5 cm, pink-tan, soft. Part B: Breast tissue measuring 3.5 x 2.5 x 1.8 cm, tan-yellow with central fibrosis.

Histologic Examination:
Part A: Sentinel lymph node shows reactive changes with prominent germinal centers. No malignant cells identified. Tested with cytokeratin immunostain - negative.

Part B: Sections show invasive ductal carcinoma with high grade nuclear features. Tumor is present adjacent to surface margin (inked blue). Central portion shows extensive necrosis. Surrounding tissue shows previous chemotherapy effect with scattered tumor cells and fibrosis. Lymphocytic infiltrate prominent throughout.

Grade: Modified Bloom-Richardson grade 3.

Margins: Tumor present at blue-inked surface, close to all other margins (within 1 mm).

Lymphovascular invasion: Present.

BREAST TISSUE FRAGMENTS - IMAGING GUIDED BIOPSY

Clinical: 38-year-old presents with abnormal findings on breast MRI - suspicious enhancement in medial aspect of right breast.

Specimen: Multiple fragments of pink-tan breast tissue totaling approximately 1.5 cm. Code 1: Medial tissue fragment 0.8 cm. Code 2: Central tissue fragment 0.6 cm. Code 3: Lateral reference tissue 0.7 cm.

Microscopic Findings:
Code 1: Shows invasive carcinoma NST (no special type) with clear cell features. Tissue composed predominantly of tumor cells with increased mitotic figures. Grade 2-3.

Code 2: Shows high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ with central comedonecrosis.

Code 3: Benign breast parenchyma with fat.

Diagnosis: Invasive carcinoma with associated DCIS.

Note: Please correlate with mammographic findings for extent of disease assessment. Recommend further imaging and staging studies.

MASTECTOMY SPECIMEN - LEFT BREAST WITH AXILLARY DISSECTION

Clinical History: 55-year-old female with 2-year history of palpable mass, now undergoing mastectomy with axillary node dissection.

Specimen: Entire breast tissue measuring 12 x 10 x 6 cm with attached axillary fat measuring approximately 8 x 7 x 4 cm. Skin paddle measures 8 x 6 cm including nipple-areolar complex. Primary tumor identified in upper outer quadrant measuring 2.8 x 2.5 x 2.2 cm, firm, grey-white.

Macroscopic Examination: Nipple inverted with associated eczematous changes. Tumor in upper outer quadrant with ill-defined borders, extending toward superior and lateral margins. Multiple axillary lymph nodes identified in fat, largest measuring 1.5 x 1.2 x 0.8 cm.

Microscopic Findings: Invasive ductal carcinoma grade 2-3. Tumor shows areas of necrosis and stromal sclerosis. Perineural invasion identified. Extensive lymphocytic infiltrate.

Lymph Node Status: Of 12 axillary lymph nodes identified, 4 are involved with metastatic carcinoma (largest deposit 8 mm).

Margins: Tumor extends to within 3 mm of surface margin, clear of deep margin.

Nipple/Areola: Cells of nipple show reactive epithelial changes, no malignancy identified.
